name: Radxa ROCK 5B
description: RK3588, premier high-performance ARM SBC alternative

release-date.comment: Pre-orders opened January 2022; broad shipments began Q2 2022.

status.comment: Still in production alongside the newer ROCK 5B+; verified 2026-04-26.

open-source-hardware.comment: Radxa releases schematic PDFs, 2D DXF outlines, and 3D STP models, but no PCB layout source / Gerber / BOM for the ROCK 5B. Per RESEARCH.md, schematics-only does not qualify as OSHW.

country-of-origin.comment: Radxa is headquartered in Shenzhen, Guangdong.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: A76 cluster up to 2.4 GHz, A55 cluster up to 1.8 GHz.

opengl-es-version.comment: Mali-G610 MC4 supports OpenGL ES 3.2 / OpenCL 2.2 / Vulkan 1.2 per Arm spec.

vulkan-support.comment: Mali-G610 supports Vulkan 1.2; mainline Panthor driver upstreamed in kernel 6.10 (2024).

hw-video-encode.comment: Up to 8K@30 encode; AV1 encode is NOT supported on RK3588.

ram-max-gb.comment: Original ROCK 5B ships in 4/8/16 GB SKUs; the larger 24/32 GB tiers are sold under the ROCK 5B+ revision.

ram-type.comment: 64-bit LPDDR4x. Some retailer sheets call it LPDDR4 generically; LinuxGizmos and CNX-Software cite LPDDR4x.

emmc.comment: eMMC connector populated optionally; sold as a module rather than soldered down.

sata.comment: No native SATA port; storage attaches via the M.2 NVMe slot or USB.

wifi.comment: No onboard Wi-Fi. Optional Radxa Wireless Module A8 (Wi-Fi 6 / BT 5.2) attaches via the M.2 E-key slot.

bluetooth.comment: No onboard Bluetooth; available only when the optional M.2 E-key wireless module is fitted (BT 5.2).

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: 2× USB 3.0 Type-A (one is USB3 OTG/HOST, one is USB3 HOST).

usb-c-ports-data.comment: 1× USB-C (USB 3.0) carries DisplayPort alt-mode and accepts USB-PD power; usable for data.

poe-support.comment: PoE pads broken out for an external PoE HAT; not built into the Ethernet port.

hdmi-outputs.comment: 2× HDMI 2.1 outputs (Type-A) up to 8K@60 / 4K@60 respectively. Plus a separate micro-HDMI INPUT — not counted.

displayport.comment: DisplayPort via USB-C alt-mode (up to 8K@30); no dedicated DP connector.

audio-jack.comment: 4-ring 3.5 mm TRRS jack with line-out and mic-in.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 40-pin header in Raspberry Pi 40-pin pinout, with I2C/SPI/UART/I2S/PWM.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Pi-compatible 40-pin pinout, but PCB is Pico-ITX (100×72 mm) so HAT mechanical mounting and EEPROM auto-config are not RPi-spec.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: M.2 M-key 2280 for NVMe SSD; M.2 E-key for the wireless module.

power-input.comment: USB Power Delivery via USB-C; also accepts 5V via the GPIO header. Vendor recommends a 30 W+ PD supply.

idle-power-w.comment: Reviewer measurement (Ethernet + eMMC, no NVMe / Wi-Fi). Other reviews report ~2 W; Jeff Geerling saw ~8 W with desktop Ubuntu and a NVMe attached.

load-power-w.comment: Stress test on bret.dk; the-diy-life logs 8–10 W full CPU load.

cooling-required.comment: RK3588 throttles under sustained load on a passive heatsink alone; Radxa's own heatsink is fanned and active cooling is the de-facto recommendation.

rtc-battery-header.comment: Onboard RTC battery connector with marked +3.3V / GND pins.

form-factor-class.comment: 100×72 mm matches the Pico-ITX form factor.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Pico-ITX mounting pattern, not Raspberry Pi standard.

weight-g.comment: Vendor does not publish a bare-board weight; not reported reliably in major reviews. Marked null per RESEARCH.md guidance to avoid guessing.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: As of kernel 6.10–6.13 (2024–2025): USB3, PCIe-attached 2.5 GbE, Mali-G610 GPU, and HDMI display work upstream. Hardware video decode (VDPU381 H.264), NPU, MIPI DSI, HDMI audio/CEC, and USB-PD are still in flux. Many users still run the BSP 6.1/5.10 kernel for full peripheral coverage; Armbian ships both a mainline (6.18) and vendor (6.1) track marked stable.

official-os-image.comment: Radxa publishes Debian Bullseye (5.10), Debian Bookworm (6.1), and Android 12 images.

distros-with-images.comment: Vendor Debian images plus first-class community images from Ubuntu (KDE/XFCE), Armbian (mainline + vendor tracks), and DietPi. Manjaro/Arch ARM/Batocera/openFyde also exist but not in this enum.

long-term-support.comment: Radxa does not publish a formal support window. Mainlining of RK3588 by Collabora and the rolling Armbian effort suggest multi-year practical support, but it is not contractually stated.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Original 4 GB launch MSRP (early 2022). Street prices in 2026 are higher and the 4 GB SKU is widely out of stock; ROCK 5B+ has effectively replaced the low end.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Original 16 GB launch MSRP. 2026 retail typically $160–$250 for the 8/16 GB variants.

available-regions.comment: Sold via Radxa's approved-partner network (Allnet China, Arace, Ameridroid, OKdo, ThinkRobotics, etc.) covering NA, EU, and APAC.

typical-lead-time.comment: Snapshot 2026-04-26: 8/16 GB SKUs intermittently in stock at major distributors; 4 GB largely sold through. Original ROCK 5B is being phased out in favor of the 5B+, so availability skews tighter than the newer model.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive vendor docs site with getting-started, hardware design, low-level dev, and OS guides; schematic PDFs published. Loses a point for the missing PCB sources and occasional staleness between the new docs site and the older wiki.

community-size.comment: Radxa is established in the second tier per RESEARCH.md thresholds (alongside Khadas/FriendlyELEC). Active forum + Armbian/r/SBCs presence.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum.radxa.com plus an official Discord.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Official heatsinks, metal cases, PoE HAT, wireless module, NVMe shields, and MIPI camera/display modules; broader than most second-tier SBCs but well below the Raspberry Pi accessory universe.
